message:
fix(process): prevent stdout/stderr pipe deadlock by reading on dedicated threads

Both run_command_with_timeout and run_command_cancellable used
child.wait_with_output() which reads stdout and stderr sequentially
after the child exits. When the child produces enough output to fill
the OS pipe buffer (~64KB on Linux), it blocks waiting for the pipe
to be drained, but the parent is waiting for the child to exit first —
creating a deadlock.

Fix by spawning dedicated reader threads for stdout and stderr
immediately after spawning the child process. Each thread reads to
completion via read_to_end and sends the result through an mpsc channel.
The parent's try_wait/kill logic then collects from the channels instead
of calling wait_with_output.

After timeout-kill, child.wait() is called (without output collection)
since the reader threads will have captured whatever was produced before
the kill signal.

Both the standard timeout path and the cancellable path receive the
same fix for consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
